title: 10 best herbal creams for jet lag

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with jet lag because of its unique ability to regulate the body's internal clock. The cream's active ingredient, valerenic acid, has a sedative effect that can help calm the nervous system and promote relaxation. This can be particularly beneficial when traveling across time zones, as it can help reduce symptoms such as insomnia, restlessness, and fatigue associated with jet lag. By promoting a sense of relaxation and calm, herbal valerian creams can help regulate sleep patterns and ease the transition back to one's normal routine.
remedy_properties: Sedative and Relaxant Properties: Valerian cream's sedative and relaxant properties can help promote a good night's sleep, allowing your body to adjust to the new time zone and reduce symptoms of jet lag.

Muscle Relaxation and Soothing Properties: The cream's muscle relaxation and soothing properties can help alleviate any physical discomfort or stiffness that may have developed during long periods of travel, making it easier to unwind and relax upon arrival.

Adaptogenic and Stress-Reducing Properties: Valerian's adaptogenic and stress-reducing properties can help regulate your body's natural rhythms and reduce feelings of anxiety, fatigue, and disorientation often experienced when traveling across time zones.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are a rich source of valepotriates, which have been shown to help regulate sleep patterns and reduce symptoms of insomnia.
Rhizomes: Valerian rhizomes contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which can help to calm the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety caused by jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried valerian root and 10g of beeswax from your supplier or local market for extraction.
Combine the dried valerian root with 200ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a double boiler for decarboxylation at 65°c for 1 hour.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and filter it to obtain 100ml of valerian-infused oil after 2 hours of maceration.
Melt the beeswax with 20g of shea butter and 10g of cocoa butter in a heat-proof bowl in a water bath at 70°c for 30 minutes.
Blend the melted mixture with the valerian-infused oil, 20ml of distilled water, and 1% vitamin e oil while whisking constantly for 5 minutes until smooth and creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with jet lag because of its ability to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels. When traveling across time zones, our bodies struggle to adjust to the new sleep-wake cycle, leading to fatigue, insomnia, and irritability. Lemon balm's calming properties help regulate the body's natural rhythms, easing the transition into a new schedule. Its soothing effects also alleviate anxiety and promote a good night's sleep, allowing your body to recover from the physical and mental demands of travel.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic and sedative properties: Lemon balm cream can help alleviate anxiety and stress caused by time zone changes, promoting a restful night's sleep.
Calming effects on the nervous system: The herb's calming effects can help regulate the body's circadian rhythms, reducing symptoms of jet lag such as insomnia, irritability, and fatigue.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm cream may also help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by travel-related changes in diet and environment, which can exacerbate jet lag symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a rich source of antioxidants and adaptogenic compounds that help to calm and balance the body's natural rhythms, making them ideal for soothing jet lag symptoms.
Buds: The buds of the lemon balm plant contain a high concentration of essential oils and flavonoids that can help to reduce stress and anxiety caused by jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried melissa officinalis leaves and stems and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine the powdered melissa officinalis with 200ml of sweet almond oil in a double boiler and heat it gently at 60°c for 30 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it infuse for 4 hours before straining it through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Mix 20g of beeswax with 40ml of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat until the beeswax is fully melted and combined.
Gradually add the infused melissa officinalis mixture to the beeswax-coconut oil mixture, stirring constantly, until it reaches a consistency suitable for cream formation after 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop creams helps with jet lag because it contains Passiflora incarnata, a natural herb that has been used for centuries to promote relaxation and reduce stress. When our body's internal clock is disrupted due to travel across time zones, the maypop cream can help calm the mind and regulate our sleep-wake cycle. Its sedative properties can induce a peaceful slumber, allowing us to adjust to the new time zone more efficiently. Additionally, the cream's adaptogenic properties help the body adapt to the new environment, reducing fatigue and discomfort caused by jet lag.
remedy_properties: Passionflower extract: Helps to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, making it easier to adjust to new time zones and sleep patterns.
Melatonin-rich herbs: Contain natural melatonin, which helps regulate sleep-wake cycles and can help alleviate insomnia caused by jet lag.
Adaptogenic properties of Ashwagandha: Help the body adapt to stress and fatigue associated with travel, allowing for a smoother transition to new time zones and reducing jet lag symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Maypop plant contain compounds that help regulate the body's natural circadian rhythms, making them ideal for soothing jet lag symptoms.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of the Maypop plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce fatigue and promote relaxation, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality passiflora incarnata flowers and dry them properly for 7-10 days to preserve their potency.
Combine dried flowers with coconut oil in a ratio of 1:3 by weight and steep the mixture for 2-4 weeks at room temperature.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove solids and discard the solids, then add beeswax and vitamin e oil.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat while stirring until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches 120-130°f, then let it cool and solidify at room temperature.
Once cooled and solidified, package the cream in airtight containers and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or freeze for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Figwort creams helps with jet lag because they contain a unique blend of herbal extracts that work together to regulate the body's natural rhythms. The figwort plant, in particular, has been used for centuries to treat fatigue and stress-related disorders. When applied topically as a cream, it helps to calm the nervous system, reducing feelings of jet lag-induced anxiety and restlessness. Additionally, the cream's antioxidant properties help to combat oxidative stress caused by long-distance travel, promoting a faster recovery from the effects of jet lag.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Figwort cream's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce swelling and discomfort caused by travel-related fatigue, making it easier for the body to adjust to new time zones.

Adaptogenic property: Figwort is an adaptogen, which means it helps the body adapt to stressors like jet lag by balancing the body's natural cortisol levels and promoting relaxation, allowing for a smoother transition into the new timezone.
Antioxidant property: Figwort cream's antioxidant properties help to protect against oxidative stress caused by long-distance travel, reducing fatigue, and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in reducing fatigue and discomfort associated with jet lag.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and terpenes, which help regulate sleep patterns and improve mood.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh scrophularia nodosa roots and wash them gently under cold running water.
Dry the roots in a warm oven at 50°c for 24 hours or use a food dehydrator.
Grind 100g of dried root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar.
Mix 20g of scrophularia nodosa powder with 80g of coconut oil and 10g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a smooth consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with jet lag because they promote relaxation and calmness, which can be disrupted by time zone changes. The calming properties of chamomile soothe the skin, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by fatigue and stress. As a natural adaptogen, chamomile also regulates the body's natural rhythms, helping to reset the circadian clock and alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as insomnia, restlessness, and grogginess.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile creams help reduce inflammation caused by jet lag, which can lead to pain and discomfort in the body.
Sedative properties: The sedative compounds in chamomile creams promote relaxation, calming the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep after a long flight.
Antioxidant properties: Chamomile creams' antioxidants help combat oxidative stress caused by travel, which can disrupt the body's natural rhythms and make it harder to adjust to new time zones.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots are rich in apigenin, an antioxidant that helps regulate sleep-wake cycles and reduce stress caused by jet lag.
Barks: Chamomile barks contain flavonoids that soothe inflammation and promote relaxation, making them effective for calming symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue and irritability.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers and dry them properly for 24 hours in the shade.
Weigh out 20 grams of dried chamomile flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the powdered chamomile with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and 10 milliliters of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the wax has melted completely and the mixture is smooth.
Pour the cooled cream into small glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to two weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams help with jet lag because of their ability to regulate sleep-wake cycles and reduce fatigue. The herb contains hyperforin, a compound that inhibits the reuptake of serotonin, a neurotransmitter responsible for modulating our body's internal clock. By increasing serotonin levels, St John's Wort creams can help reset the body's natural rhythm, making it easier to adjust to new time zones. This natural remedy can also alleviate symptoms such as insomnia and daytime grogginess, allowing you to feel more refreshed and energized after long-haul flights.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has been shown to regulate circadian rhythms, helping to adjust to new sleep-wake cycles after travel across time zones.

Hypotensive effect: The active compounds in St John's Wort cream, such as hypericin and flavonoids, have a relaxing effect on blood vessels, which can help alleviate jet lag-related fatigue and promote relaxation.
Antioxidant properties: St John's Wort has antioxidant activity that helps to reduce oxidative stress caused by travel, such as inflammation, fatigue, and irritability.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St. John's Wort contain high levels of hypericin, which has been shown to help regulate sleep patterns and alleviate symptoms of jet lag.
Flowers: St. John's Wort flowers are rich in flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that can help soothe skin irritations caused by jet lag-related fatigue and stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers at dawn on a sunny day in mid-june.
Combine 50g of dried hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers with 200ml of olive oil in a double boiler.
Steep the mixture for 4 hours or overnight, then strain through cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Add 10g of beeswax and 5g of vitamin e oil to the liquid extract, then heat gently until the wax melts.
Pour the warm mixture into small jars and let it cool before applying topically to ease jet lag symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with jet lag because they promote relaxation and reduce stress, which are common symptoms of travel fatigue. The calming scent of lavender has a natural sedative effect on the mind and body, helping to regulate sleep patterns and ease feelings of jet lag-related insomnia. By applying an English lavender cream topically, individuals can experience a sense of calm and tranquility, making it easier to adjust to new time zones and get a good night's rest after a long flight.
remedy_properties: Sedative and relaxing effects: Lavender's calming properties can help reduce stress, anxiety, and insomnia caused by jet lag, promoting a good night's sleep.
Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds can help soothe and calm the body, reducing fatigue and muscle tension associated with jet lag.
Stress-reducing and adaptogenic effects: Lavender's adaptogenic properties can help regulate the body's natural rhythms and reduce stress, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, lavender roots help reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, making them ideal for calming symptoms of jet lag.
Seeds: Containing a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, lavender seeds possess sedative properties that can aid in regulating sleep patterns disrupted by travel.
remedy_recipe: Weigh and combine dried lavandula angustifolia flowers, glycerin, and distilled water in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Steam distill the mixture at 100°c for 20 minutes, then allow it to cool down to room temperature.
Blend cooled infusion with cocoa butter, beeswax, and sweet almond oil, heating the mixture to 60°c until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into sterilized tubes or jars, allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Label and store the creams in a cool, dark place for up to six months, using as needed to alleviate jet lag symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats creams helps with jet lag because they promote relaxation, calmness, and a sense of serenity. The soothing properties of oats help to ease anxiety and stress caused by travel, allowing the body to naturally adjust to its new surroundings. Additionally, herbal ingredients such as chamomile and lavender in oat creams can aid in regulating sleep patterns, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep despite the disruption of time zones. This helps to combat fatigue and promote a restful night's sleep, alleviating the symptoms of jet lag.
remedy_properties: Soothing and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Oats' anti-inflammatory compounds, such as avenanthramides, help to reduce stress and inflammation caused by travel fatigue, promoting relaxation and rest.

Calming and Sedative Effects: The presence of herbs like lavender, chamomile, and valerian in oats creams can induce a calming effect on the mind and body, helping to alleviate jet lag-related anxiety and insomnia.

Hydrating and Moisturizing Properties: Oats' emollient properties help to lock in moisture, soothing dry skin and eyes often affected by air travel, and providing relief from fatigue-induced dehydration.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in beta-glucans, which help to regulate the body's circadian rhythms and reduce stress associated with jet lag.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe skin irritation caused by travel fatigue and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Combine 250 grams of dried oat straw avena sativa flowers with 500 milliliters of organic sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Infuse the mixture by placing it in a saucepan and gently heating it over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container, pressing on solids to extract maximum liquid.
Add 20 grams of beeswax pellets to the strained oil and stir until melted completely at room temperature within one hour.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for two hours before pouring it into decorative tubes or jars for use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with jet lag because they contain extracts that have been shown to regulate sleep patterns and calm the mind. The creams' anti-inflammatory properties can reduce fatigue and stiffness caused by long flights, while their sedative properties promote a sense of relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep in a new time zone. Additionally, the creams' ability to soothe skin irritations and dryness can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag-induced stress and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Melatonin: Some herbal creams contain melatonin, a hormone that regulates sleep-wake cycles. Melatonin supplements can help the body adjust to new time zones and improve sleep quality.
Valerian root: Valerian root has been traditionally used as a natural sedative and relaxant, which can help reduce stress and promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep despite jet lag.
Ginkgo biloba: Ginkgo biloba is believed to improve blood flow to the brain, which may help alleviate symptoms of fatigue, headaches, and dizziness commonly associated with jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help alleviate fatigue and joint pain caused by jet lag.
Barks: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help regulate circadian rhythms and improve sleep quality.
remedy_recipe: Grind 10 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Mix the ground ginkgo biloba powder with 20 grams of coconut oil and 5 grams of beeswax in a heat-proof bowl until well combined.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the wax is fully melted.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to around 120°f (49°c) before whipping it with a hand mixer for 5 minutes.
Pour the whipped cream into sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator at a temperature of 3-5°c for up to 6 months or use within 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with jet lag because they promote digestive regularity, which is often disrupted by long-haul flights. The calming properties of peppermint also help to ease stress and anxiety associated with travel, allowing for a smoother transition to the new time zone. Additionally, peppermint's refreshing and invigorating scent can help increase alertness and reduce fatigue, making it easier to adjust to local bedtime routines.
remedy_properties: Digestive Soothing Properties: Peppermint's calming effects on the digestive system can help ease stomach discomfort, nausea, and bloating caused by jet lag, allowing for a more restful sleep.

Pain Relieving Properties: Peppermint's analgesic properties can help alleviate headaches, migraines, and muscle tension often experienced during travel and time zone changes, promoting relaxation and reducing discomfort.

Stomach Regulating Properties: Peppermint's ability to regulate digestion and reduce symptoms like bloating, gas, and indigestion can help adjust the body to new food sources and schedules, making it easier to transition into a new time zone.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of peppermint are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing fatigue and stress caused by jet lag.
Leaves: Peppermint leaves contain menthol and menthone, which help to soothe digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as nausea and stomach upset.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves mentha x piperita and rinse them thoroughly with cool filtered water.
Dry the peppermint leaves in a single layer on paper towels for at least 24 hours or until crispy dry.
Grind dried peppermint leaves into a fine powder using an electric spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix ground peppermint powder with beeswax pellets and coconut oil in a double boiler set over simmering water.
Whip the mixture continuously for approximately 10 minutes or until it reaches a smooth creamy consistency suitable for application on skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Broad-leaved lime creams helps with jet lag because they contain a unique blend of herbs that work together to regulate the body's natural rhythms. The cream's calming properties help to reduce stress and anxiety associated with travel, while its adaptogenic ingredients help to normalize the body's internal clock. This allows travelers to adjust to their new time zone more easily, minimizing the symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue, insomnia, and disorientation.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Tilia cordata cream has antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by jet lag, promoting relaxation and reducing fatigue.

Anxiolytic and sedative effects: The herb contains flavonoids and terpenes that have been shown to exhibit anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) and sedative effects, helping to alleviate the symptoms of insomnia and restlessness associated with jet lag.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Tilia cordata cream has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce swelling and pain caused by jet lag-related fatigue, making it easier to recover from travel.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help regulate circadian rhythms and reduce symptoms of jet lag.
Buds: High in essential oils and resins, providing anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties to soothe skin irritation caused by jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest tilia platyphyllos flowers and leaves in a clean environment free of contaminants.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator at 55°c for 6 hours or air drying for 10 days.
Combine 200g of dried tilia platyphyllos with 400ml of organic coconut oil and 100ml of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax has melted and the mixture is smooth and consistent.
Strain the cream through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar, allowing it to cool before storing in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange creams helps with jet lag because they contain a unique combination of herbs and essential oils that help regulate the body's internal clock. The citrus and bitter notes in these creams stimulate the production of serotonin, a neurotransmitter that regulates sleep-wake cycles. As the body adjusts to new time zones, the bitter orange cream provides a gentle push towards natural sleep patterns, reducing the discomfort and fatigue associated with jet lag. This allows travelers to adapt more easily to their new environment and arrive feeling refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic compounds: Herbal bitter orange creams contain adaptogenic compounds like flavonoids and limonoids, which help the body adapt to new environments and rhythms, reducing stress and fatigue caused by jet lag.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in herbal bitter orange creams, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation caused by travel-related stressors like changes in diet, environment, and sleep patterns.

Melatonin-boosting compounds: Some herbal bitter orange creams contain melatonin-boosting compounds like valerian root and hops, which can help regulate the body's natural circadian rhythms and promote a restful night's sleep, even during times of disruption caused by jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain flavonoids and coumarins that help regulate sleep-wake cycles, making them effective in treating jet lag-related insomnia.
Seeds: Bitter orange seeds contain sesquiterpenes that have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety associated with jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality citrus x aurantium peel and dry it in a warm oven at 50°c for 4 hours.
Mix dried peel with 100g of coconut oil and 20g of beeswax, stirring until the mixture is smooth.
Add 5g of vitamin e oil and 2g of lavender essential oil to the mixture, blending well.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60°c for 30 minutes, whipping constantly until it thickens.
Pour the final cream into sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator at 3°c for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree creams helps with jet lag because they promote natural hormone balance, which is disrupted by crossing time zones. The cream's active ingredient, vitex agnus-castus, stimulates the production of hormones that regulate sleep-wake cycles, helping to adjust to new schedules and reduce fatigue. Additionally, chaste tree creams can alleviate stress and anxiety caused by travel, further aiding in a smooth transition back to your regular routine. By supporting hormonal balance, chaste tree creams can help you feel refreshed and revitalized despite the challenges of jet lag.
remedy_properties: Hormonal Balancing Properties: Chaste tree cream helps regulate hormonal imbalances caused by jet lag, which can disrupt sleep patterns and overall well-being.

Adaptogenic Properties: The cream's adaptogenic effects enable the body to cope better with stress and fatigue associated with travel across time zones, promoting relaxation and reducing symptoms of jet lag.

Anxiolytic and Sedative Properties: Chaste tree extract has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and promote a sense of calm, making it easier to fall asleep despite disruptions caused by jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chaste tree roots are rich in essential oils that help regulate sleep patterns and reduce symptoms of jet lag.
Barks: The barks of the chaste tree contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate fatigue and stress associated with jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried vitex agnus castus leaves and flowers from a reliable source in quantities of approximately 20 grams.
Combine the harvested herb with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or jojoba oil at a ratio of 1:5.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for one hour to facilitate the infusion process without excessive heat damage.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate the herb residue from the extracted oil, discarding solids and retaining 100 milliliters of oil.
Mix the strained oil with 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler until fully melted, then pour into glass containers for storage at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn creams helps with jet lag because they contain adaptogenic herbs that help regulate body rhythms. The calming properties of hawthorn soothe the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety caused by travel disruptions. Additionally, hawthorn's adaptogenic effects allow it to normalize cortisol levels, which can become disrupted due to changes in sleep patterns. By using a hawthorn cream, individuals can promote relaxation, reduce fatigue, and ease the transition back into their regular routine after crossing time zones.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Hawthorn's adaptogenic properties help the body adjust to changes in environment, stress, and rhythm, which can alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue and disorientation.

Cardio-protective compounds: Hawthorn's cardio-protective compounds, such as flavonoids and procyanidins, may help regulate blood flow and circulation, which can reduce symptoms of fatigue, insomnia, and anxiety associated with jet lag.

Antioxidant properties: Hawthorn's antioxidant properties help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by travel-induced inflammation and stress, which can contribute to fatigue, headaches, and other symptoms of jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help soothe and calm the skin after long flights.
Barks: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness and swelling caused by jet lag-related fatigue.
Seeds: High in omega-3 fatty acids, which nourish and regenerate the skin while reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried crataegus monogyna leaves and stems from local pharmacies or online suppliers.
Combine the dried herb with 100ml of cold-pressed olive oil in a dark glass jar and store for 4 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another container, discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Mix 20g of beeswax pellets with 40ml of coconut oil and 30ml of vitamin e oil in a double boiler.
Add the crataegus monogyna-infused olive oil to the melted wax mixture and stir until fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle creams helps with jet lag because they contain silymarin, a potent antioxidant that promotes liver health and detoxification. When we travel across time zones, our bodies struggle to adjust to new sleep-wake cycles, leading to fatigue, insomnia, and disorientation. Silymarin in milk thistle creams supports the body's natural rhythms by gently stimulating the liver's natural detox processes, helping to eliminate excess stress hormones and toxins that can disrupt our circadian rhythms. This natural cream can help alleviate jet lag symptoms and promote a smoother transition to our new time zone.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Silymarin, a flavonoid compound found in milk thistle, has antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by time zone changes, promoting faster adaptation to new environments.

Silibinin: Silibinin, another bioactive compound present in milk thistle, exhibits anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate symptoms such as fatigue, headaches, and joint pain often experienced during jet lag.

Cardiotonic properties: Milk thistle's cardiotonic properties can help regulate the body's natural circadian rhythms by supporting the heart's normal functioning and promoting a sense of calmness, reducing symptoms like insomnia and anxiety commonly associated with jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silymarin, a potent antioxidant that helps protect against liver damage caused by jet lag-related fatigue and stress.
Seeds: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as headaches and insomnia.
remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum plants and dry them properly in a warm well-ventilated area for at least 7 days.
Combine 20g of dried silybum marianum flowers with 200ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 30 minutes to infuse the herbal extract into the carrier oil then let it cool down completely.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids and retaining the filtered liquid.
Whip 100g of beeswax with 200ml of the infused oil until smooth and creamy adding 10 drops of lavender essential oil for a calming effect.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with jet lag because they contain natural ingredients that promote digestion, reduce inflammation, and regulate cortisol levels. The soothing properties of dandelion can help ease stress and anxiety associated with traveling across time zones. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory compounds may help alleviate jet lag symptoms such as fatigue, headaches, and joint pain. By addressing these underlying issues, herbal dandelion creams can help your body adjust to its new environment more quickly, reducing the discomfort of jet lag.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Dandelion's adaptogenic properties help regulate the body's response to stress, including the stress caused by jet lag, allowing for a smoother transition to a new time zone.
Digestive soothing effects: The anti-inflammatory and soothing compounds in dandelion cream can calm digestive issues that often accompany jet lag, such as nausea, bloating, and cramps.
Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's antioxidant properties help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by travel fatigue, which can exacerbate symptoms of jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing inulin, which helps regulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of jet lag-related gastrointestinal distress.
Barks: Rich in triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe skin irritation caused by jet lag-induced fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in the morning after the dew has dried off for freshness and potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 3-4 hours or until crispy.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for optimal extraction.
Mix 20 grams of the powdered taraxacum officinale with 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler on low heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a glass jar, discarding any solids and letting it cool before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen creams helps with jet lag because they contain menthol, which has a natural ability to ease fatigue and promote relaxation. When used topically on the skin, menthol can help stimulate blood flow and reduce muscle tension, making it easier for the body to adjust to new time zones. Additionally, wintergreen's calming properties can soothe any irritability or stress associated with traveling, allowing you to more easily adapt to a new sleep schedule. As a result, herbal wintergreen creams can provide a natural solution for alleviating jet lag symptoms and promoting a smoother travel experience.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol has a cooling effect on the skin, which can help to stimulate blood flow and reduce feelings of fatigue and grogginess often associated with jet lag.
Glycosides: Wintergreen contains glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing discomfort and promoting relaxation.

Essential Oils: The essential oils present in wintergreen, such as methyl salicylate and camphor, have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to promote relaxation and reduce stress associated with jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: Wintergreen roots have been traditionally used to treat fatigue and stress, making them a popular choice for medicinal creams aimed at alleviating the symptoms of jet lag.

Barks: The bark of the wintergreen plant contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, both of which are beneficial for combating jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves and dry them in a warm dark place for at least seven days.
Combine two tablespoons of dried gaultheria procumbens leaves with one cup of coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in the refrigerator for 14 hours to allow the herbs to infuse into the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass jar discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil.
Whip one cup of shea butter with two tablespoons of the reserved infused oil until smooth and creamy for use as a cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops creams helps with jet lag because of their unique ability to regulate the body's natural sleep-wake cycle. The calming properties of hops work to soothe an overactive mind, allowing for a deeper and more restful sleep. Additionally, hops' sedative effects help to counteract the stimulating effects of caffeine and other substances that can disrupt sleep patterns. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress, herbal hop creams can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag, making it easier to adjust to new time zones and wake up feeling refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Herbal hops creams contain adaptogens like humulone, which help the body adapt to stressors like travel disruptions, promoting relaxation and reducing feelings of anxiety and fatigue.

Sedative and calming effects: The sedative compounds in herbal hops creams, such as linalool, can help regulate sleep patterns and induce a sense of calm, making it easier to fall asleep despite jet lag.

Anxiolytic properties: Herbal hops creams contain anxiolytic compounds like xanthohumol, which can reduce anxiety levels and promote relaxation, helping individuals cope with the stress and discomfort associated with jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help regulate sleep patterns and reduce symptoms of jet lag.
Buds: Contain flavonoids and terpenes that have a calming effect on the body and mind, promoting relaxation and reducing fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh humulus lupulus leaves and flowers, drying them for at least four hours in the shade.
Combine 20 grams of dried humulus lupulus with 40 grams of coconut oil, heating mixture over low heat for one hour.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and stir until fully incorporated, allowing the mixture to cool completely overnight.
Mix in 5 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 2 milliliters of vitamin e oil, whipping the cream until smooth and creamy.
Transfer the cream to sterilized jars, storing them in a cool dark place for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi creams helps with jet lag because they promote relaxation, calmness, and clarity of mind. The herb Brahmi, also known as Bacopa monnieri, has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for centuries to enhance cognitive function, memory, and mental well-being. When applied topically, it can help regulate the body's internal clock, reducing stress and anxiety caused by time zone changes. As a result, brahmi creams can help improve sleep quality, reduce fatigue, and increase energy levels, making them an effective natural remedy for jet lag relief.
remedy_properties: Cerebroprotective properties: Brahmi's ability to protect the brain from damage helps alleviate symptoms of jet lag, such as fatigue and mental fogginess.
Anxiolytic properties: Brahmi's calming effects can help reduce stress and anxiety caused by time changes, making it easier to adjust to a new sleep schedule.
Memory-enhancing properties: Brahmi's ability to improve memory and cognitive function can aid in the process of adjusting to a new environment and reducing the disorienting effects of jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: Brahmi roots have been traditionally used to calm the mind and promote relaxation, making them an excellent choice for jet lag relief.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of brahmi contain a high concentration of antioxidants and adaptogenic compounds that help regulate sleep patterns and reduce fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried bacopa monnieri leaves from a trusted supplier in a ratio of 10:1.
Grind the bacopa monnieri leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 30 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of the powdered bacopa monnieri with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil in a double boiler for 15 minutes.
Heat the mixture to 60°c to infuse the oil with the herbal extract without exceeding 10 minutes.
Strain and filter the cream through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into sterilized containers within 10 minutes.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for jet lag are topical products infused with natural herbs and essential oils designed to alleviate symptoms of jet lag, such as fatigue, insomnia, and disorientation. These creams work by promoting relaxation and calming the mind and body, helping to regulate sleep patterns and reduce travel-related stress. Examples include creams containing valerian root, lavender oil, and melatonin, which have been shown to improve sleep quality and duration. By using these herbal creams, travelers can enjoy a more restful journey and arrive at their destination feeling refreshed and revitalized, ultimately improving their overall well-being and daily routine.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat jet lag with herbal creams is by using soothing and calming formulas that help regulate your body's circadian rhythms. Look for creams containing herbs like valerian root, chamomile, and lavender, which promote relaxation and reduce stress. Apply these creams topically before bed to ease into a restful sleep, allowing your body to adjust to the new time zone naturally.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jet lag that are treated with herbal creams are typically related to circadian rhythm disruptions or sleep disorders. Herbal creams containing ingredients like valerian root, chamomile, and lavender may help alleviate symptoms of insomnia, restlessness, and fatigue often experienced during times of travel or seasonal changes. These natural remedies can promote relaxation, reduce stress, and regulate the body's internal clock to improve overall sleep quality.
